cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question step by step. The question is about Wu Jinyou and which statement is true about him. The options are A to D. First, I need to find all the information in the given text related to Wu Jinyou. Let me go back to the article and locate the part where he is mentioned. Looking through the article, there's a paragraph that says: "Wu Jinyou, head of the Polar Research Institute of China, and the group's leader, said, 'I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here. I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us.'" So, from this, we know that Wu Jinyou is the head of the Polar Research Institute of China and the leader of the group. His statement mentions that he hopes the students are touched by the devotion and spirit of the researchers and that some might become polar researchers. Now, let's look at the answer choices: A) He is a graduate from Dalian Marinetime University. The article mentions Zheng Li as a student from Dalian Marinetime University, but there's no mention of Wu Jinyou attending that university. So A is likely false. B) He is the president of Dalian Marinetime University. Again, there's no information linking Wu Jinyou to the presidency of Dalian Marinetime University. The article states he's head of the Polar Research Institute, not related to the university's leadership. So B is probably false. C) He thinks highly of the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. In his quote, Wu Jinyou mentions that he hopes the students are touched by the devotion and spirit of the researchers. This implies that he values their devotion and spirit, which would mean he thinks highly of them. So C seems true. D) He is sure that all the ten students will make good polar researchers. Wu Jinyou's statement is about hoping that "some" of them will
